zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan le Tort於大約1220年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特時，她的父親Laurence Le Tort22歲，母親Mrs. Laurence le Tort20歲。她於1240年與Simon de Raleigh在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。她去世於1315年在英國戴文享年96歲。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Tort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English, Old French tort(e) ‘twisted, crooked’. Compare Robert Tortesmains, 1169 in Pipe Rolls (Yorks) ‘twisted hands’; Deudon’ cum pedibus tortis, 1191 in Pipe Rolls (London) ‘with twisted feet’.

2 English: nickname from Middle English tourte (Old French torte), denoting a tart or a kind of coarse brown bread, for someone who baked or sold such food items. Compare John Roke, tourtbaker, 1355 in Patent Rolls .

variants.name: Pipe Rolls
meanings: 1 English: nickname, relationship name from Middle English * but(te), Old English * butta ‘(something or someone) short and stumpy’, also used as a personal name up until about 1200. The Hants surname (Middle English le But) possibly refers to the man called But, 1170 in Pipe Rolls (Hants), who may also be commemorated in Buttsash (‘Butt’s ash tree’) in Fawley (Hants). The place-name is first recorded in 1212 as Bottesesse; see Hampshire Place-Names , which notes that a William le But was resident in Fawley in 1327. Some of the post-medieval bearers may belong under (2).

2 English: (i) locative name for someone who lived by a tree stump. This sense is not recorded in English before 1601 but is probably much earlier; see (1). (ii) locative name for someone who lived near a mound, from Middle English but(te).

variants.name: Patent Rolls
meanings: English: nickname, locative name, variant of Hulk with post-medieval excrescent -s. The now extinct Hulk may either mean ‘very big man’ (compare Simon Hulkebon, 1316 in Patent Rolls ), or be from Middle English hulk ‘shed’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan le Tort was born about 1220, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Laurence Le Tort, was 22 and her mother, Mrs. Laurence le Tort, was 20. She married Simon de Raleigh in 1240, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. She died in 1315, in Devon, England, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan le Tort est née environ 1220, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Laurence Le Tort, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Laurence le Tort, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Simon de Raleigh en 1240, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Elle est décédée en 1315, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan le Tort um 1220 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Laurence Le Tort 22 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Laurence le Tort 20. Sie hat 1240 in Somerset, England Simon de Raleigh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Sie starb 1315 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan le Tort è nata il circa 1220 nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Laurence Le Tort, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Laurence le Tort, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Simon de Raleigh nel 1240, nel Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli. Morì nel 1315, nel Devon, England, all'età di 96 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan le Tortが約1220年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Laurence Le Tortは22歳，母Mrs. Laurence le Tortは20歳であった。1240年にイギリスソメルセトでSimon de Raleighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼女は1315年にイギリスデボンで96歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan le Tort(이)가 대략 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Laurence Le Tort(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Laurence le Tort(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1240년 영국 서머세트에서 Simon de Raleigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1315년 영국 데폰에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan le Tort nasceu aproximadamente 1220, em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Laurence Le Tort, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Laurence le Tort, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Simon de Raleigh em 1240, em Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1315, em Devon, Inglaterra, com 96 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan le Tort родилась около 1220 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Laurence Le Tort было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Laurence le Tort, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Simon de Raleigh в 1240 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Она умерла в 1315 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 96 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan le Tort nació aproximadamente 1220, en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Laurence Le Tort, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Mrs. Laurence le Tort, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Simon de Raleigh en 1240, en Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos. Murió en 1315, en Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 96 años.
